metadata.title: Core SPC for Human Prothrombin Complex Products

rules.rule_text: Prothrombin complex products are indicated for treatment of bleeding and perioperative prophylaxis in acquired deficiency of prothrombin complex factors (e.g. VKA-induced) when rapid correction is required, and for congenital deficiency of any vitamin K dependent factor when purified specific factor product is not available.
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment of bleeding and perioperative prophylaxis of bleeding in acquired deficiency of the prothrombin complex coagulation factors, such as deficiency caused by treatment with vitamin K antagonists, or in case of overdose of vitamin K antagonists, when rapid correction of the deficiency is required ... Treatment of bleeding and perioperative prophylaxis in congenital deficiency of any of the vitamin K dependent coagulation factors when purified specific coagulation factor product is not available.

rules.rule_text: Treatment must be initiated under supervision of a specialist experienced in coagulation disorders. INR monitoring during treatment is mandatory.
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment should be initiated under the supervision of a physician experienced in the treatment of coagulation disorders. ... As these recommendations are empirical and recovery and the duration of effect may vary, monitoring of INR during treatment is mandatory.

rules.rule_text: For VKA reversal, the dose depends on the INR before treatment and the targeted INR. Product-specific approximate doses (ml/kg) must be provided at different initial INR levels (2-2.5, 2.5-3, 3-3.5, >3.5).
rules.verbatim_quote: The dose will depend on the INR before treatment and the targeted INR. In the following table approximate doses (ml/kg body weight of the reconstituted product) required for normalisation of INR at different initial INR levels are given.

rules.rule_text: In acquired VKA-induced deficiency, PCC should only be used when rapid correction of prothrombin complex levels is necessary (major bleeding or emergency surgery). Otherwise, reducing VKA dose and/or administering vitamin K is usually sufficient.
rules.verbatim_quote: In patients with acquired deficiency of the vitamin K dependent coagulation factors (e.g. as induced by treatment of vitamin K antagonists), {Invented name of the product} should only be used when rapid correction of the prothrombin complex levels is necessary, such as major bleeding or emergency surgery. In other cases, reduction of the dose of the vitamin K antagonist and/or administration of vitamin K is usually sufficient.

rules.rule_text: Patients receiving VKA may have an underlying hypercoagulable state, and infusion of PCC may exacerbate this.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients receiving a vitamin K antagonist may have an underlying hypercoaguable state and infusion of human prothrombin complex may exacerbate this.

rules.rule_text: In congenital deficiency of any vitamin K dependent factor, the specific purified coagulation factor product should be used when available. PCC is only a substitute when specific product is unavailable.
rules.verbatim_quote: In congenital deficiency of any of the vitamin K dependent factors, specific coagulation factor product should be used when available.

rules.rule_text: There is a risk of thrombosis or DIC with PCC, particularly with repeated dosing. The risk is higher in treatment of isolated FVII deficiency since other vitamin K dependent factors with longer half-lives may accumulate to supranormal levels. Close monitoring is required in patients with coronary heart disease, liver disease, peri/post-operative status, neonates, and DIC risk patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: There is a risk of thrombosis or disseminated intravascular coagulation when patients, with either congenital or acquired deficiency are treated with human prothrombin complex particularly with repeated dosing. The risk may be higher in treatment of isolated factor VII deficiency, since the other vitamin K dependent coagulation factors, with longer half-lives, may accumulate to levels considerably higher than normal.

rules.rule_text: PCC products neutralise the effect of VKA treatment. This is the only known drug interaction.
rules.verbatim_quote: Human prothrombin complex products neutralise the effect of vitamin K antagonist treatment, but no interactions with other medicinal products are known.

rules.rule_text: High doses of PCC have been associated with myocardial infarction, DIC, venous thrombosis, and pulmonary embolism. Overdose risk of thromboembolic complications or DIC is enhanced.
rules.verbatim_quote: The use of high doses of human prothrombin complex products has been associated with instances of myocardial infarction, disseminated intravascular coagulation, venous thrombosis and pulmonary embolism. Therefore, in case of overdose, the risk of development of thromboembolic complications or disseminated intravascular coagulation is enhanced.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must include a PK table providing half-life information for each coagulation factor (II, VII, IX, X).
rules.verbatim_quote: A table providing half-life information should be included. ... Coagulation factor: Factor II [product specific], Factor VII [product specific], Factor IX [product specific], Factor X [product specific]
rules.exceptions: It may be acceptable that some data are provided from literature.

rules.rule_text: PCC strength must be expressed as content of coagulation factor IX per container. Composition must declare IU of all four factors (II, VII, IX, X) per container and per ml after reconstitution.
rules.verbatim_quote: {(Invented) name of product <strength, expressed as content of coagulation factor IX per container>} ... The product nominally contains the following IU of the human coagulation factors tabled below: Coagulation factor II, Coagulation factor VII, Coagulation factor IX, Coagulation factor X

rules.rule_text: PCC must not be mixed with other medicinal products. Only provided injection/infusion sets should be used due to risk of coagulation factor adsorption.
rules.verbatim_quote: This medicinal product must not be mixed with other medicinal products. ... Only the provided <injection> <infusion> sets should be used because treatment failure can occur as a consequence of coagulation factor adsorption to the internal surface of some injection/infusion equipment.
